objective: Bootstrap the narrow parent-owned #612 hygiene child that reconciles closed child #629 out of active docs/PROGRESS.md state while preserving honest completed-history visibility.
scope_boundary: Planning-only hygiene child lane under issue #612.
Owns docs/PROGRESS.md reconciliation for closed child #629 only, plus issue-local artifacts.
Must preserve honest completed-history visibility for #629 while removing its active IN PROGRESS representation.
Must not widen into OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, #629 implementation surfaces, #632 drift, or broader #612 enforcement work unless scope is explicitly widened first.
out_of_scope: Reopening issue #629 fallback-log schema or workflow implementation.
Reopening issue #632 planning-authority implementation or mirror drift.
Broader #612 validator, workflow, or routing enforcement.
Edits to docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md or docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md unless matching stale #629 drift is concretely proven and the lane is widened.
research_summary: Parent-lane audit and specialist review confirmed that OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md was already reconciled by issue #633, but docs/PROGRESS.md still lists closed child #629 as active IN PROGRESS work. The narrowest honest owner is a new parent-owned #612 hygiene child limited to docs/PROGRESS.md reconciliation for #629 only, preserving completed-history visibility without widening into the separate stale #632 row or broader #612 enforcement surfaces.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local planning packet and keep #636 bounded to the single stale #629 docs/PROGRESS.md row.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, planning execution scope, handoff, review packet, cross-review note, validation artifact, and issue-scoped review artifact.
Record the exact stale #629 row and the requirement to preserve honest completed-history visibility while removing active state.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names docs/PROGRESS.md and issue-local artifacts as the only owned surfaces.
The packet states that #629 must leave IN PROGRESS and remain visible honestly as completed history.
The packet preserves OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, #612, #629, and #632 substantive surfaces as external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only issue-local planning artifacts during bootstrap.
Implementation remains blocked until planning validation and alternate-family review complete.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify whether the stale closed-#629 mirror row belongs to a new parent-owned #612 hygiene child and confirm the narrowest valid write surface.
specialist_reviews.summary: The stale #629 row is real in docs/PROGRESS.md, while OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md was already corrected by #633, so the narrowest honest owner is a new parent-owned #612 hygiene child limited to docs/PROGRESS.md only.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the lane stays limited to stale #629 docs/PROGRESS.md mirror hygiene and does not reopen broader #612 work or absorb other stale rows.
specialist_reviews.summary: The acceptable boundary is docs/PROGRESS.md plus issue-local artifacts only, preserving honest completed-history visibility for #629 and excluding OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, #629 implementation, #632 drift, and broader #612 enforcement work.

alternate_model_review.summary: Sanctioned alternate-family review passed for the planning-only #636 packet with no blocking findings; the only residual note is that implementation should choose an explicit completed-history presentation for the #629 row when editing docs/PROGRESS.md.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #636 is authorized for the bounded docs/PROGRESS.md hygiene slice only: reconcile closed child #629 out of active IN PROGRESS state while preserving honest completed-history visibility, plus issue-local artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the single-row docs/PROGRESS.md reconciliation for closed child #629, prove the bounded mirror-hygiene change, and close the slice without widening into OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, #632, or broader #612 surfaces.
blocked_on: Issue #612 remains the parent fallback-enforcement lane and is not closed by #636 alone.
Issue #633 already owns OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md hygiene and must not be reopened here.
Issue #632 drift remains out of scope for this bounded lane.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Issue #636 remains bounded to docs/PROGRESS.md stale-#629 mirror hygiene and issue-local artifacts only.
AC2: docs/PROGRESS.md no longer lists closed issue #629 as active IN PROGRESS work and preserves honest completed-history visibility.
AC3: Issue #636 preserves OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, #612, #629, and #632 substantive surfaces as external boundaries and does not claim broader #612 closure.
AC4: The bounded implementation proof chain is reconciled through the issue-local validation and closeout artifacts.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit outside docs/PROGRESS.md and issue-local artifacts is a material deviation unless matching stale #629 mirror drift is first proven and scope is widened explicitly.
Any edit to OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md is a material deviation from issue #636 scope.
Any edit to #629 implementation surfaces is a material deviation.
Any edit to #632 mirror drift or implementation surfaces is a material deviation.
Any claim that #636 closes broader #612 enforcement is a material deviation.
